qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated local admin API permits full process control
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is about unauthenticated loopback admin endpoints in `admin.go` and `caddyconfig/load.go`, not the advisory's swallowed CA-file errors in `modules/caddytls/connpolicy.go` that make mTLS client verification fail open.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described mTLS fail-open root cause.
evidence: The advisory describes two `return nil` sites in `modules/caddytls/connpolicy.go` during `ClientAuthentication.provision()` that swallow CA-file errors and leave `ClientCAs` unset during mTLS verification.

The only qualifying finding instead reports unauthenticated local access to the plaintext admin API, specifically `POST /stop` and `POST /load`, with code locations in `repo/admin.go` and `repo/caddyconfig/load.go`.
No final qualifying finding mentions CA certificate file parsing, `TrustedCACerts`, `clientauth.ca`, `ConfigureTLSConfig()`, or a TLS client-certificate trust-boundary bypass.
